text.title: Release Train Coordination Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the release-train-coordination methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale. The terminal `skip-this-methodology` rule is the safety valve invoked by the decision tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Release window MUST be a fixed weekday+hour with at least 2 weeks of lookahead.
rule.rationale: Floating windows kill predictability and force ad-hoc work.

rule.statement: Code-freeze window MUST name start hour + duration + exception path.
rule.rationale: Implicit freezes cause last-minute merges that bypass review.

rule.statement: Branch model (trunk / release / hotfix) MUST be enumerated; ad-hoc branches forbidden.
rule.rationale: Mixed models cause merge conflicts and lost commits.

rule.statement: Captain rotation MUST be fair (no person scheduled twice in 6 trains).
rule.rationale: Repeated captain duty causes burnout and lowers release quality.

rule.statement: Each train MUST end with a 15-min retro logged to `reviews/`.
rule.rationale: Retros are the only feedback loop that keeps the train tuned.

rule.statement: Author MUST stop and route elsewhere when the decision-tree resolves to a skip leaf; partial application of this methodology against an unfit input is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Forcing the methodology onto a mismatched input produces low-quality artefacts and erodes trust in the corpus.
